13.31: Or what king, going to make war against another king, sitteth not down first, and consulteth whether he be able with ten thousand to meet him that cometh against him with twenty thousand?
13.32: Or else, while the other is yet a great way off, he sendeth an ambassage, and desireth conditions of peace.
13.33: So therefore, whosoever he be of you that renounceth not all his possessions, he cannot be my disciple.
13.34: Salt is good: but if the salt have lost his savour, wherewith shall it be seasoned?
13.35: It is neither fit for the land, not yet for the dunghill; but men cast it out. He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
13.36: Now all the publicans and sinners were coming near unto him, to hear him.
13.37: And the Pharisees and scribes murmured, saying, This man receiveth sinners, and eateth with them.
13.38: And he spake this parable unto them, saying,
13.39: What man of you, having an hundred sheep, if he lose one of them, doth not leave the ninety and nine in the wilderness, and go after that which is lost, until he find it?
13.40: And when he hath found it, he layeth it on his shoulders, rejoicing.
13.41: And when he cometh into the house, he calleth together his friends and neighbours, saying unto them, Rejoice with me; for I have found my sheep which was lost.
13.42: I say unto you, that likewise joy shall be in the heaven over one sinner that repenteth, more than over ninety and nine righteous persons, which have no need of repentance.
13.43: Either what woman having ten drachmas, if she lose one drachma, doth not light a lamp, and sweep the house, and seek diligently till she find it?
13.44: And when she hath found it, she calleth her friends and her neighbours together, saying, Rejoice with me; for I have found the drachma which I had lost.
13.45: Likewise, I say unto you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner that repenteth.

14.1: And he said also unto his disciples, There was a certain rich man, which had a steward; and the same was accused unto him as wasting his property.
14.2: And he called him, and said unto him, How is it that I hear this of thee? render the account of thy stewardship; for thou canst not be a steward any longer.
14.3: Then the steward said within himself, What shall I do? for my lord taketh away from me the stewardship: I have not strength to dig; to beg I am ashamed.
14.4: I know what I will do, that, when I am put out of the stewardship, they may receive me into their houses.
14.5: And he called every one of his lord’s debtors unto him, and said unto the first, How much owest thou unto my lord?
14.6: And he said, An hundred baths of oil. And he said unto him, Take thy bond, and sit down quickly, and write fifty.
14.7: Then said he to another, And how much owest thou? And he said, An hundred cors of wheat. And he said unto him, Take thy bond, and write fourscore.
14.8: And the lord commended the steward of unrighteousness, because he did wisely: for the sons of this age are unto their own generation wiser beyond the children of the light.
14.9: And I say unto you, Make to yourselves friends of the mammon of unrighteousness; that, when ye fail, they may receive you into eternal habitations.
14.10: He that is faithful in a very little is faithful in much: and he that is unrighteous in very little is unrighteous also in much.
14.11: If therefore ye have not been faithful in the unrighteous mammon, who will commit to your trust the true riches?
14.12: And if ye have not been faithful in that which is another ‘s, who shall give you that which is mine?
14.13: No servant can serve two masters: for while one will be pleased the other must needs be displeased. Ye cannot serve God and mammon.
14.14: And the Pharisees also, who were lovers of money, heard all these things: and they sneered at him.
14.15: And he said unto them, Ye are they which make yourselves righteous in the sight of men; but God knoweth your hearts: for that which is highly esteemed among men is abomination in the sight of God.
14.16: Think not that I am come to fulfil the law, or the prophets: I am not come to fulfil, but to destroy.
14.17: The law and the prophets were until John: since that time the kingdom of God is preached, and every man presseth into it.
14.18: And it is easier for heaven and earth to pass, than one tittle of my words to fail.
14.19: Everyone that putteth away his wife and marrieth another, committeth adultery: and everyone that marrieth one that is put away from a husband also committeth adultery.
14.20: Again, ye have heard that it hath been said by them of old time, Thou shalt not forswear thyself, but shalt perform unto the Lord thine oaths:
14.21: But I say unto you, Swear not at all; neither by heaven; for it is God’s throne:
14.22: Nor by the earth; for it is his footstool: neither by Jerusalem; for it is the city of the great King.
14.23: Neither shalt thou swear by thy head, because thou canst not make one hair white or black.
14.24: But let your communication be, Yea, yea; Nay, nay: for whatsoever is more than these cometh of evil.
14.25: There was a certain rich man, with the name Nineue, and he was clothed in purple and fine linen, faring sumptuously every day:
14.26: And there was a certain beggar named Lazarus, which was laid at his gate, full of sores,
14.27: And desiring to be fed with the crumbs which fell from the rich man’s table: but even the dogs came and licked his sores.
14.28: And it came to pass, that the beggar died, and was carried by the angels into Abraham’s bosom: the rich man also died, and was buried.
14.29: And in hell he lifted up his eyes, being in torments, and seeth Abraham afar off, and Lazarus in his bosom.
14.30: And he cried and said, Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus, that he may dip the tip of his finger in water, and cool my tongue; for I am in anguish in this flame.
14.31: But Abraham said, Son, remember that thou in thy lifetime receivedst thy good things, and likewise Lazarus evil things: but now he is comforted, and thou art in anguish.
14.32: And besides all these things, between us and you there is a great gulf fixed: so that they which wish to cross from hence to you may not be able; neither can they from thence pass through to us.
14.33: But he said, I pray thee therefore, father, that thou wouldest send him to my father’s house:
14.34: For I have five brethren; that he may testify unto them, lest they also come into this place of torment.
14.35: Abraham saith unto him, They have Moses and the prophets; let them hear them.
14.36: And he said, Nay, father Abraham: but if one from the dead went unto them, they will repent.
14.37: And he said unto him, If they hear not Moses and the prophets, not even if one from the dead arose, will they be persuaded.

15.1: Then said he unto the disciples, It is impossible but that offences will come: but woe unto him, through whom they come!
15.2: It were better for him if he had not been born, or if a millstone were hanged about his neck and he were cast into the sea, than that he should offend one of these little ones,
15.3: Take heed to yourselves: But if thy brother trespass against thee, rebuke him; and if he repent, forgive him.
15.4: And if he trespass against thee seven times in the day, and seven times in the day turn again to thee, saying, I repent; thou shalt forgive him.
15.5: Again I say unto you, That if two of you shall agree on earth as touching any thing that they shall ask, it shall be done for them of my Father which is in heaven.
15.6: For where two or three are gathered together in my name, there am I in the midst of them.
15.7: Therefore is the kingdom of heaven likened unto a certain king, which would take account of his servants.
15.8: And when he had begun to reckon, one was brought unto him, which owed him ten thousand talents.
15.9: But forasmuch as he had not to pay, his lord commanded him to be sold, and his wife, and children, and all that he had, and payment to be made.
15.10: The servant therefore fell down, and worshipped him, saying, Lord, have patience with me, and I will pay thee all.
15.11: Then the lord of that servant was moved with compassion, and loosed him, and forgave him the debt.
15.12: But the same servant went out, and found one of his fellowservants, which owed him an hundred pence: and he laid hands on him, and took him by the throat, saying, Pay me that thou owest.
15.13: And his fellowservant fell down at his feet, and besought him, saying, Have patience with me, and I will pay thee all.
15.14: And he would not: but went and cast him into prison, till he should pay the debt.
15.15: So when his fellowservants saw what was done, they were very sorry, and came and told unto their lord all that was done.
15.16: Then his lord, after that he had called him, said unto him, O thou wicked servant, I forgave thee all that debt, because thou desiredst me:
15.17: Shouldest not thou also have had compassion on thy fellowservant, even as I had pity on thee?
15.18: And his lord was wroth, and delivered him to the tormentors, till he should pay all that was due unto him.
15.19: So likewise shall my heavenly Father do also unto you, if ye from your hearts forgive not every one his brother their trespasses.
15.20: And the apostles said unto the Lord, Increase our faith.
15.21: And the Lord said, If ye had faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye would say unto this mulberry tree, Be thou plucked up-rooted, and be thou planted in the sea; and it would have obeyed you.
15.22: But which of you, having a servant plowing or keeping cattle, will say unto him by and by, when he is come from the field, Go and sit down to meat?
15.23: And will not rather say unto him, Make ready wherewith I may sup, and gird thyself, and serve me, till I have eaten and drunken; and afterward thou shalt eat and drink?
15.24: Doth he thank that servant because he did the things that were commanded him? I trow not.
15.25: So likewise ye, when ye shall have done all those things which are commanded you.
15.26: And it came to pass, as he went to Jerusalem, that he passed through the midst of Samaria and Galilee.
15.27: And as he entered into a certain village, there met him ten men that were lepers, which stood afar off:
15.28: And they lifted up their voices, and said, Jesus, Master, have mercy on us.
15.29: And when he saw them, he said unto them, Go shew yourselves unto the priests. And it came to pass, that, as they went, they were cleansed.
15.30: And many lepers were in Israel in the days of Elisha the prophet, but none of them was cleansed, but Naaman the Syrian.
15.31: And one of them, when he saw that he was healed, turned back, and with a loud voice glorified God,
15.32: And fell down on his face at his feet, giving him thanks: and he was a Samaritan.
15.33: And Jesus answering said, Were not the ten cleansed? but where are the nine?
15.34: There are not found that returned to give glory to God but this stranger.
15.35: And he said unto him, Arise, go thy way: thy faith hath made thee whole.
15.36: And when he was questioned by the Pharisees, when the kingdom of God cometh, he answered them and said, The kingdom of God cometh not with observation:
15.37: Neither shall they say, Lo here! or, lo there! for, behold, the kingdom of God is within you.
15.38: And he said unto the disciples, The days will come, when ye shall desire to see one of the days of the Son of man, and ye shall not see it.
15.39: And they shall say to you, Lo here!, or, lo there! go not away, nor follow after them.
15.40: For as the lightning, that lighteneth out of the one part under heaven, shineth unto the other part under heaven; so shall also the Son of man be.
15.41: But first must he suffer many things, and be rejected of this generation.
15.42: And as it came to pass in the days of Noah, so shall it be also in the days of the Son of man.
15.43: They ate, they drank, they married, they were given in marriage, until the day that Noah entered into the ark, and the flood came, and destroyed them all.
15.44: Likewise also as it came to pass in the days of Lot; they ate, they drank, they bought, they sold, they planted, they builded;
15.45: But in the day that Lot went out from Sodom it rained fire and brimstone from heaven, and destroyed them all.
15.46: Even thus shall it be in the day when the Son of man is revealed.
15.47: In that day, he which shall be upon the housetop, and his goods in the house, let him not go down to take them away: and he that is in the field, let him likewise not return back.
15.49: Whosoever shall seek to save his life shall lose it; and whosoever shall lose his life shall preserve it.
15.50: I tell you, in that night there shall be two men in one bed; the one shall be taken, and the other shall be left.
15.51: Two women shall be grinding together; the one shall be taken, and the other left.
15.52: Two men shall be in the field; the one shall be taken, and the other left.
15.53: And they answered and said unto him, Where, Lord? And he said unto them, Wheresoever the body is, there will the eagles be gathered together.

16.1: And he spake also a parable unto them on the neccessity always to pray, and not to faint; Saying,
16.2: there was in a city a judge, which feared not God, and regarded not man:
16.3: And there was a certain widow in that city; and she came unto him, saying, Right me from my opponent at law.
16.4: And he would not for a time: but afterward he said within himself, Though I fear not God, and regard not man;
16.5: Yet because this widow gives me trouble, I will right her, lest by her coming to the end she weary me.
16.6: And the Lord said, Hear what the judge of the unrighteous saith.
16.7: And shall not God perform the righting of his elect, which cry unto him day and night, though he be long suffering over them?
16.8: I say unto you that he will perform the righting of them speedily. Nevertheless when the Son of man cometh, shall he find the faith on the earth?
16.9: And he spake this parable unto certain which trusted in themselves that they were righteous, and accounted nothing of the rest;
16.10: Two men went up into the temple to pray; the one a Pharisee, and the other a publican.
16.11: The Pharisee stood and prayed thus: God, I thank thee, that I am not as other men are, extortioners, unrighteous, adulterers, or even as this publican.
16.12: I fast twice in the week, I give tithes of all that I get.
16.13: And the publican, standing afar off, would not lift up even his eyes unto heaven, but smote upon his breast, saying, God be propitiated to me the sinner.
16.14: I say unto you, this man went down to his house made righteous rather than that one: for every one that exalteth himself shall be humbled; and he that humbleth himself shall be exalted.
16.15: And they were bringing unto him also the infants, that he should touch them: but when the disciples saw it, they rebuked them.
16.16: But Jesus called them unto him, and said, Suffer the little children to come unto me, and hinder them not: for of such is the kingdom of God.
16.17: Verily I say unto you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little child shall in no wise enter into it.
16.18: One said unto him, saying, Good Master, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?
16.19: And Jesus said unto him, Call not thou me good. One is good, God the Father.
16.20: I know the commandments – Do not kill, Do not commit adultery, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Honour thy father and thy mother.
16.21: All these have I have observed from my youth up.
16.22: But when Jesus heard these things, he said unto him, Yet one thing is lacking for thee: sell all things, as many as thou hast, and distribute unto the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, follow me.
16.23: And when he heard these things, he became exceeding sorrowful: for he was very rich.
16.24: And when Jesus saw him become exceeding sorrowful, he said, How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of God!
16.25: For it is easier for a camel to enter through a needle’s eye, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.
16.26: And they that heard it said, Who then can be saved?
16.27: And he said, The things which are impossible with men are possible with God.
16.28: Then Peter said, Lo, we have left all, and followed thee.
16.29: And he said unto them, Verily I say unto you, There is no man that hath left house, or parents, or brethren, or wife, or children, for the kingdom of God’s sake,
16.30: Who shall not receive manifold more in this time, and in the coming age eternal life.
16.31: And they understood none of these things: and his saying was his from them, neither knew they the things which were spoken.
16.32: And it came to pass that as he was come nigh unto Jericho, a certain blind man sat by the way side begging:
16.33: And when he heard the multitude passing by, he asked what this might be.
16.34: And they told him, that Jesus passeth by.
16.35: And he cried, saying, Jesus, thou Son of David, have mercy on me.
16.36: And they which went before rebuked him, that he might be silent: but he cried so much the more, Thou Son of David, have mercy on me.
16.37: And Jesus stood still, and commanded him to be brought unto him: and when he was come near, he asked him, saying,
16.38: What wilt thou that I shall do unto thee? And he said, Lord, that I may receive my sight.
16.39: And Jesus said unto him, Receive thy sight: thy faith hath saved thee.
16.40: And immediately he received his sight, and followed him, glorifying God: and all the people, when they saw it, gave praise unto God.

17.1: And Jesus entered and passed through Jericho.
17.2: And, behold, a man called by the name Zacchaeus, and he was a chief publican, and he was rich.
17.3: And he sought to see Jesus who he was; and could not from the multitude, because he was little of stature.
17.4: And he ran before, and climbed up into a sycomore tree to see him: for he was going to pass through that way.
17.5: And when Jesus came to the place, he looked up, and saw him, and said unto him, Zacchaeus, make haste, and come down; for to day I must abide at thy house.
17.6: And he made haste, and came down, and received him joyfully.
17.7: And when they saw it, they all murmured, saying, That he was gone to lodge with a man that is a sinner.
17.8: And Zacchaeus stood still, and said unto the Lord; Behold, Lord, the half of my goods I give to the poor; and if I have taken any thing from any man by false accusation, I restore fourfold.
17.9: And Jesus said unto him, This day is salvation come to this house,
17.10: For the Son of man is come to seek and to save that which was lost.
17.11: And as they heard these things, he added and spake a parable, because he was nigh to Jerusalem, and because they supposed that the kingdom of God was going to appear immediately.
17.12: He said therefore, A certain nobleman went into a distant country to receive for himself a kingdom, and to return.
17.13: And he called his ten servants, and delivered them ten pounds, and said unto them, Trade till I come.
17.14: But his citizens hated him, and sent his embassy after him, saying, We do not wish this man to reign over us.
17.15: And it came to pass, that when he was returned, having received the kingdom, that he commanded these servants to be called unto him, to whom he gave the money, that he might know what trade every man had accomplished.
17.16: And the first came before him, saying, Lord, thy mina hath wrought ten minas more.
17.17: And he said unto him, Well, thou good servant: because thou wast faithful in a very little, be thou holding authority over ten cities.
17.18: And the second came, saying, Lord, thy mina hath gained five minas.
17.19: And another came, saying, Lord, behold, here is thy mina, which I have kept laid up in a napkin:
17.20: For I feared thee, because thou art an austere man: thou takest up that thou layedst not down, and reapest that thou sowest not.
17.21: And he saith unto him, Out of thine own mouth will I judge thee, thou wicked servant. Knewest thou that I was an austere man, taking up that I laid not down, and reaping that I sowed not?
17.22: Wherefore then gavest thou not my money into the bank, and I would have come and exacted it with interest?
17.23: And he said unto them that stood by, Take from him the mina, and give it to him that hath ten minas.
17.24: And they said unto him, Lord, he hath ten minas.
17.25: For I say unto you, That unto every one which hath shall be given; and from him that hath not, even that he hath shall be taken away from him.
17.26: But those mine enemies, that did not wish me to reign over them, bring hither, and slay them before me. And cast ye the unprofitable servant unto the outer darkness, there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
17.27: And when he had thus spoken, he went before, ascending up to Jerusalem.
17.28: And when he was come nigh, even now at the descent of the mount of Olives, the whole multitude of the disciples began to rejoice and praise God with a loud voice for all the mighty works that they had seen;
17.29: Saying, Blessed be the King that cometh in the name of the Lord: peace in heaven, and glory in the highest.
17.30: And some of the Pharisees from among the multitude said unto him, Master, rebuke thy disciples.
17.31: And he answered and said unto them, I tell you that, if these should hold their peace, the stones would immediately cry out.
17.32: And he was teaching daily in the temple. But the chief priests and the scribes and the chief of the people sought to destroy him,
17.33: And could not find what they might do: for all the people were very attentive to hear him.

18.1: And it came to pass, that on one of those days, as he taught the people in the temple, and preached the gospel, the chief priests and the scribes came upon him with the elders,
18.2: And spake unto him, saying, Tell us, by what authority doest thou these things? or who is he that gave thee this authority?
18.3: And he answered and said unto them, I will also ask you one thing; and answer me:
18.4: The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men?
18.5: And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say, Why then believed ye him not?
18.6: But and if we say, Of men; all the people will stone us: for they be persuaded that John was a prophet.
18.7: And they answered, that they could not tell whence it was.
18.8: And Jesus said unto them, Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.
18.9: Whosoever shall fall upon that stone shall be broken; but on whomsoever it shall fall, it will grind him to powder.
18.10: And the chief priests and the scribes the same hour sought to lay hands on him; and they feared the people: for they perceived that he had spoken this parable against them.
18.11: And they watched him, and sent forth spies, feigning themselves to be righteous men, that they might take hold of his speech, in order to deliver him up unto the rule and authority of the governor.
18.12: And they asked him, saying, Teacher, we know that thou sayest and teachest rightly, and acceptest not a countenance, but of a truth teachest the way of God.
18.13: Is it lawful for us to give tribute unto Caesar, or not?
18.14: But he perceived their craftiness, and said unto them, Why tempt ye me?
18.15: Shew me a denarius. Whose image and superscription hath it? They answered and said, Caesar’s.
18.16: And he said unto them, Render therefore unto Caesar the things of Caesar, and unto God the things of God.
18.17: And they were not able to take hold of his saying before the people: and they marvelled at his answer, and held their peace.
18.18: And there came to him certain of the Sadducees, they which deny that there is any resurrection; and they asked him, saying,
18.19: Teacher, Moses wrote unto us, that if any man’s brother die, having a wife, and he die childless, that his brother should take the wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.
18.20: There were therefore seven brethren: and the first took a wife, and died childless.
18.21: And the second took her to wife, and he died childless.
18.22: And the third took her; and likewise the seven also left no children, and died.
18.23: Last of all the woman died also.
18.24: In the resurrection, therefore, whose wife of them does she become? for the seven had her as a wife.
18.25: And Jesus answered and said unto them, The sons of this age marry, and are given in marriage:
18.26: But they whom God accounted worthy of that age, to obtain the resurrection from the dead, neither marry, nor are given in marriage:
18.27: Neither can they die any more: for they are equal unto the angels; and are the sons of God, being the children of the resurrection.
18.28: Then certain of the scribes answering said, Teacher, thou hast well said.
18.29: And they did not any longer venture to ask him anything.
18.30: And he said unto them, How say they that the Christ is David’s son?
18.31: And David himself saith in the book of Psalms, The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand,
18.32: Till I make thine enemies the footstool of thy feet.
18.33: David therefore calleth him Lord, and how is he then his son?
18.34: And when all the people were hearing him, he said unto his disciples,
18.35: Beware of the scribes, which desire to walk in long robes, and love salutations in the markets, and the first seats in the synagogues, and the first rooms at feasts;
18.36: Which eat up the houses of widows, and for a pretext make long prayers: the same shall receive greater condemnation.

19.1: And he looked up, and saw the rich men that were casting their gifts into the treasury.
19.2: And he saw also a certain poor widow casting in thither two lepta.
19.3: And he said, Of a truth I say unto you, that this poor widow hath cast in more than they all:
19.4: For all these did out of their super-abundance cast in unto the offerings of God: but she out of her want hath cast in all the living that she had.
19.5: And as some spake about the temple, that it was adorned with goodly stones and offerings, he said,
19.6: As for these things which ye behold, the days will come, in the which there shall not be left a stone upon a stone, that shall not be thrown down.
19.7: And they asked him, saying, Teacher, when then shall these things be? and what shall be the sign, when these things are going to take place?
19.8: And he said, See that ye be not led astray: for many shall come in my name, saying, I am he; and the time is come near: go ye not therefore after them.
19.9: And when ye shall hear of wars and tumults, be not terrified: for these things must first come to pass; but the end is not immediately.
19.10: Then said he unto them, Nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom:
19.11: And great earthquakes shall be in divers places, and famines, and pestilences; and terrors and great signs shall there be from heaven.
19.12: But before all these, they shall lay their hands on you, and persecute you, delivering you up to the synagogues, and into prisons, being brought before kings and rulers for my name’s sake.
19.13: And it shall turn out to you for a testimony.
19.14: Settle it therefore in your hearts, not to meditate before how to answer:
19.15: For I will give you a mouth and wisdom, which all those opposed to you shall not be able to gainsay nor withstand.
19.16: And ye shall be delivered up even by parents, and brethren, and kinsfolks, and friends; and they shall put some of you to death.
19.17: And ye shall be hated of all men for my name’s sake.
19.18: In your patience possess ye your souls.
19.19: But when ye shall see Jerusalem compassed with armies, then know that her desolation is come near.
19.20: But woe unto them that are with child, and to them that give suck, in those days! for there shall be great distress in the land, and wrath upon this people.
19.21: And they shall fall by the edge of the sword, and shall be led away captive into all nations: and Jerusalem shall be trodden down of the Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles be fulfilled.
19.22: And there shall be signs in sun, and moon, and stars; and upon the earth distress of nations, with perplexity; the sea and waves roaring;
19.23: Men fainting for fear, and for expectation of the things which are coming on the world: for the powers of the heavens shall be shaken.
19.24: And then shall they see the Son of man coming in a cloud with great power.
19.25: And when these things begin to come to pass, then look up, and lift up your heads; for your redemption draweth nigh.
19.26: And he spake to them a parable; Behold the fig tree, and all the trees;
19.27: When they already produce fruit, ye see it, and know your own selves that summer is already near.
19.28: So likewise ye, when ye see these things come to pass, know ye that the kingdom of God is near.
19.29: Verily I say unto you, The heaven and the earth shall in no wise pass away, till all things be accomplished.
19.30: The heaven and the earth shall pass away: but my words shall in no wise pass away.
19.31: And take heed to yourselves, lest at any time your hearts be weighed down with surfeiting, and drunkenness, and cares of this life, and that day come upon you unawares.
19.32: For as a snare shall it come on all them that dwell on the face of the whole earth.
19.33: Watch ye therefore, and pray always, that ye may be accounted worthy to escape all these things that shall come to pass, and to stand before the Son of man.
19.34: And every day he was teaching in the temple; and at night he went out, and lodged on the mountain that is called Olives.
19.35: And all the people came early in the morning to him in the temple to hear him.
19.36: And every man went unto his own house.
19.37: Jesus went unto the mount of Olives.
19.38: And early in the morning he came again into the temple, and all the people came unto him; and he sat down, and taught them.
19.39: And the scribes and Pharisees brought unto him a woman taken in adultery; and when they had set her in the midst,
19.40: They say unto him, Master, this woman was taken in adultery, in the very act.
19.41: Now Moses in the law commanded us, that such should be stoned: but what sayest thou?
19.42: This they said, tempting him, that they might have to accuse him. But Jesus stooped down, and with his finger wrote on the ground, as though he heard them not.
19.43: So when they continued asking him, he lifted up himself, and said unto them, He that is without sin among you, let him first cast a stone at her.
19.44: And again he stooped down, and wrote on the ground.
19.45: And they which heard it, being convicted by their own conscience, went out one by one, beginning at the eldest, even unto the last: and Jesus was left alone, and the woman standing in the midst.
19.46: When Jesus had lifted up himself, and saw none but the woman, he said unto her, Woman, where are those thine accusers? hath no man condemned thee?
19.47: She said, No man, Lord. And Jesus said unto her, Neither do I condemn thee: go, and sin no more.

20.1: Now the feast of unleavened bread drew nigh, which is called the Passover.
20.2: And the chief priests and scribes sought how they might kill him; for they feared the people.
20.3: Then entered Satan into Judas surnamed Iscariot, being of the number of the twelve.
20.4: And he went his way, and communed with the chief priests and captains, how he might betray him unto them.
20.5: And they were glad, and covenanted to give him money.
20.6: And he promised, and sought opportunity to betray him unto them in the absence of the multitude.
20.7: Now before the feast of the passover, when Jesus knew that his hour was come that he should depart out of this world unto the Father, having loved his own which were in the world, he loved them unto the end.
20.8: And supper being ended, the devil having now put into the heart of Judas Iscariot, Simon’s son, to betray him;
20.9: Jesus knowing that the Father had given all things into his hands, and that he was come from God, and went to God;
20.10: He riseth from supper, and laid aside his garments; and took a towel, and girded himself.
20.11: After that he poureth water into a bason, and began to wash the disciples’ feet, and to wipe them with the towel wherewith he was girded.
20.12: Then cometh he to Simon Peter: and Peter saith unto him, Lord, dost thou wash my feet?
20.13: Jesus answered and said unto him, What I do thou knowest not now; but thou shalt know hereafter.
20.14: Peter saith unto him, Thou shalt never wash my feet. Jesus answered him, If I wash thee not, thou hast no part with me.
20.15: Simon Peter saith unto him, Lord, not my feet only, but also my hands and my head.
20.16: Jesus saith to him, He that is washed needeth not save to wash his feet, but is clean every whit: and ye are clean, but not all.
20.17: For he knew who should betray him; therefore said he, Ye are not all clean.
20.18: So after he had washed their feet, and had taken his garments, and was set down again, he said unto them, Know ye what I have done to you?
20.19: Ye call me Master and Lord: and ye say well; for so I am.
20.20: If I then, your Lord and Master, have washed your feet; ye also ought to wash one another’s feet.
20.21: For I have given you an example, that ye should do as I have done to you.
20.22: Verily, verily, I say unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord; neither he that is sent greater than he that sent him.
20.23: If ye know these things, happy are ye if ye do them.
20.24: I speak not of you all: I know whom I have chosen: but that the scripture may be fulfilled, He that eateth bread with me hath lifted up his heel against me.
20.25: Now I tell you before it come, that, when it is come to pass, ye may believe that I am he.
20.26: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that receiveth whomsoever I send receiveth me; and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me.
20.27: Then the day of unleavened bread came, when the passover must be sacrificed.
20.28: And he said unto Peter and the rest, Go and make ready that we may eat the passover.
20.29: And they said unto him, Where wilt thou that we make ready?
20.30: And he said unto them, Behold, when ye have entered into the city, a man shall meet you, bearing a pitcher of water; follow him into the house whereinto he goeth.
20.31: And ye shall say unto the master of the house, The Teacher saith unto thee, Where is the lodging, where I may eat the passover with my disciples?
20.32: And he shall shew you a large upper room furnished: there make ready.
20.33: And they went, and found as he had said unto them: and they made ready the passover.
20.34: And when the hour was come, he sat down, and the twelve apostles with him.
20.35: And he said unto them, With desire I have desired to eat this passover with you before I suffer:
20.36: And he took bread, and gave thanks, and brake it, and gave unto them, saying, This is my body which is given for you: this do in remembrance of me.
20.37: And he took the cup, and gave thanks, and said, Take this, and divide it among yourselves:
20.38: Likewise also the cup after supper, saying, This cup is the testament in my blood, which is shed for you.
20.39: For I say unto you, I will not drink of the fruit of the vine, until the kingdom of God shall come.
20.40: But, behold, the hand of him that betrayeth me is with me on the table.
20.41: And the Son of man indeed goeth, as it hath been determined: but woe unto that man through whom he is betrayed!
20.42: And they began to dispute with themselves, which of them it was that was going to do this thing.
20.43: And there was also a contention among them, which of them is accounted to be greater.
20.44: And he said unto them, The kings of the nations have lordship over them; and they that have authority over them are called benefactors.
20.45: But ye shall not be so: but he that is the greater among you, let him be as the younger; and he that leadeth as he that serveth.
20.46: For whether is greater- he that reclineth at meat, or he that serveth? is not he that reclineth? But I am in the midst of you as he that serveth.
20.47: Ye are they which have continued with me in my temptations.
20.48: And I appoint unto you even as my Father hath appointed unto me, A kingdom.
20.49: That ye may eat and drink at my table, and sit on thrones judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
20.50: Therefore, when he was gone out, Jesus said, Now is the Son of man glorified, and God is glorified in him.
20.51: If God be glorified in him, God shall also glorify him in himself, and shall straightway glorify him.
20.52: Little children, yet a little while I am with you. Ye shall seek me: and as I said unto the Jews, Whither I go, ye cannot come; so now I say to you.
20.53: A new commandment I give unto you, That ye love one another; as I have loved you, that ye also love one another.
20.54: By this shall all men know that ye are my disciples, if ye have love one to another.
20.55: And the Lord said, Simon, Simon, behold, Satan demanded you, that he might sift you as wheat:
20.56: But I entreated for thee, that thy faith may not fail: and once thou hast turned again, establish thy brethren.
20.57: And he said unto him, Lord, I am ready to go with thee, both into prison, and to death.
20.58: And he said, I tell thee, Peter, the cock shall not crow at all to-day, before that thou shalt thrice deny that thou knowest me.
20.59: Then said he unto them, But now, he that hath a purse, let him take it, and likewise his scrip: and he that hath no sword, let him sell his garment, and buy one.
20.60: And they said, Lord, behold, here are two swords. And he said unto them, It is enough.

21.1: And he came out, and went, as he was wont, to the mount of Olives; and his disciples also followed him.
21.2: And when he was at the place, he said unto them, Pray that ye enter not into temptation.
21.3: And he was withdrawn from them about a stone’s cast, and kneeled down, and prayed,
21.4: Saying, Father, if thou be willing, remove this cup from me: nevertheless not my will, but thine, be done.
21.5: And there appeared an angel unto him from heaven, strengthening him.
21.6: And being in an agony he prayed more earnestly: and his sweat was as it were great drops of blood falling down to the ground.
21.7: And when he rose up from prayer, and was come to his disciples, he found them sleeping for sorrow,
21.8: And said unto them, Why sleep ye? rise and pray, lest ye enter into temptation.
21.9: And while he yet spake, behold a multitude, and he that was called Judas, one of the twelve, went before them, and drew near unto Jesus to kiss him.
21.10: But Jesus said unto him, Judas, betrayest thou the Son of man with a kiss?
21.11: When they which were about him saw what would follow, they said unto him, Lord, shall we smite with the sword?
21.12: Then Jesus said unto the chief priests, and captains of the temple, and the elders, which were come to him, Be ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and staves?
21.13: When I was daily with you in the temple, ye stretched forth no hands against me: but this is your hour, and the power of darkness.
21.14: Then they seized him, and led him, and brought him into the high priest’s house. But Peter was following afar off.
21.15: And when they had kindled a fire in the midst of the court, and had sat down together, Peter sat in the midst of them.
21.16: And a certain maid seeing him as he sat towards the light, looked steadfastly upon him, and said, This man also was with him.
21.17: And he denied him, saying, Woman, I know him not.
21.18: And after a little while another saw him, and said, Thou art also of them. And Peter said, Man, I am not.
21.19: And about the space of one hour after another confidently affirmed, saying, Of a truth this fellow also was with him: for he is a Galilaean.
21.20: And Peter said, Man, I know not what thou sayest. And immediately, while he yet spake, the cock crew.
21.21: And the Lord turned, and looked upon Peter. And Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said unto him, Before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice.
21.22: And Peter went out, and wept bitterly.
21.23: And the men that held Jesus mocked him, and smote him.
21.24: And when they had blindfolded him, they struck him on the face, and asked him, saying, Prophesy, who is it that smote thee?
21.25: And many other things blasphemously spake they against him.
21.26: And as soon as it was day, the elders of the people and the chief priests and the scribes came together, and led him into their council, saying,
21.27: Art thou the Christ? tell us. And he said unto them, If I tell you, ye will not believe:
21.28: And if I also ask you, ye will not answer me, nor let me go.
21.29: Hereafter shall the Son of man sit on the right hand of the power of God.
21.30: Then said they all, Art thou then the Son of God? And he said unto them, Ye say that I am.
21.31: And they said, What need we any further witness? for we ourselves have heard of his own mouth.

22.1: And the whole company arose, and brought him before Pilate.
22.2: And they began to accuse him, saying, We found this fellow perverting the nation, and destroying the law and the prophets, and forbidding to give tribute to Caesar, and turning away the women and children, saying that he himself is Christ a King.
22.3: And Pilate asked him, saying, Art thou the King of the Jews? And he answered him and said, Thou sayest.
22.4: And Pilate said to the chief priests and the multitudes, I find no fault in this man.
22.5: And they were urgent, saying, He stirreth up the people, teaching throughout all Judaea, having begun from Galilee to this place.
22.6: But when Pilate heard of Galilee, he asked whether the man were a Galilaean.
22.7: And when he knew that he was from Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent him up to Herod, who himself also was at Jerusalem at that time.
22.8: And when Herod saw Jesus, he was exceeding glad: for he was of a long time desirous to see him, because he had heard many things about him; and was hoping to see some sign done by him.
22.9: And he questioned him in many words; but he answered him nothing.
22.10: And the chief priests and the scribes stood vehemently accusing him.
22.11: And Herod with his soldiers set him at nought, and mocked him, and arraying him in a gorgeous robe, sent him back to Pilate.
22.12: And Pilate and Herod became friends with each other that very day: for before they were at enmity between themselves.
22.13: And Pilate called together the chief priests and the rulers and the people, and said unto them,
22.14: Ye brought this man unto me, as one that perverteth the people: and, behold, I examined him before you, and found no fault in this man of what ye charge against him:
22.15: No, nor yet Herod: for I sent you to him; and, lo, nothing worthy of death hath been done by him.
22.16: I will therefore chastise him, and release him.
22.17: For of necessity he must release one unto them at the feast.
22.18: But they cried out all at once, saying, Away with this man, and release unto us Barabbas:
22.19: One who for a certain insurrection made in the city, and for murder, had been cast into prison.
22.20: Pilate therefore spake again to them, wishing to release Jesus.
22.21: But they cried, saying, Crucify him, crucify him.
22.22: And he said unto them the third time, Why, what evil hath this man done? I have found no cause of death in him: I will therefore chastise him, and let him go.
22.23: But they were urgent with loud voices, asking that he might be crucified. And the voices of them and of the chief priests prevailed.
22.24: Then Pilate therefore took Jesus, and scourged him.
22.25: And the soldiers platted a crown of thorns, and put it on his head, and they put on him a purple robe,
22.26: And said, Hail, King of the Jews! and they smote him with their hands.
22.27: Pilate therefore went forth again, and saith unto them, Behold, I bring him forth to you, that ye may know that I find no fault in him.
22.28: Then came Jesus forth, wearing the crown of thorns, and the purple robe. And Pilate saith unto them, Behold the man!
22.29: When the chief priests therefore and officers saw him, they cried out, saying, Crucify him, crucify him. Pilate saith unto them, Take ye him, and crucify him: for I find no fault in him.
22.30: The Jews answered him, We have a law, and by our law he ought to die, because he made himself the Son of God.
22.31: When Pilate therefore heard that saying, he was the more afraid;
22.32: And went again into the judgment hall, and saith unto Jesus, Whence art thou? But Jesus gave him no answer.
22.33: Then saith Pilate unto him, Speakest thou not unto me? knowest thou not that I have power to crucify thee, and have power to release thee?
22.34: Jesus answered, Thou couldest have no power at all against me, except it were given thee from above: therefore he that delivered me unto thee hath the greater sin.
22.35: And from thenceforth Pilate sought to release him: but the Jews cried out, saying, If thou let this man go, thou art not Caesar’s friend: whosoever maketh himself a king speaketh against Caesar.
22.36: When Pilate therefore heard that saying, he brought Jesus forth, and sat down in the judgment seat in a place that is called the Pavement, but in the Hebrew, Gabbatha.
22.37: And it was the preparation of the passover, and about the sixth hour: and he saith unto the Jews, Behold your King!
22.38: But they cried out, Away with him, away with him, crucify him. Pilate saith unto them, Shall I crucify your King? The chief priests answered, We have no king but Caesar.
22.39: And Pilate gave sentence that their request should be done.
22.40: And he released unto them him that for insurrection and murder had been cast into prison, whom they had desired; but Jesus he delivered up to their will.
22.41: And as they led him away, they laid hold upon one Simon, a Cyrenian, that was coming out of the country, and laid on him the cross, to bear it after Jesus.
22.42: And there followed him a great company of the people, and of women, who also were bewailing and lamenting him.
22.43: But Jesus turning unto them said, Daughters of Jerusalem, weep not for me, but weep for yourselves, and for your children.
22.44: For, behold, the days are coming, in the which they shall say, Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that bare not, and the breasts that gave not suck.
22.45: Then shall they begin to say to the mountains, Fall on us; and to the hills, Cover us.
22.46: For if they do these things in the green tree, what shall be done in the dry?
22.47: And there were also two other, malefactors, led with him to be put to death.
22.48: And when they were come to the place, which is called Calvary, there they crucified him, and the malefactors, one on the right hand, and the other on the left.
22.49: Then said Jesus, Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do. And they parted his raiment, and cast lots.
22.50: And the people stood beholding. And the rulers also with them derided him, saying, He saved others; let him save himself, if he be Christ, the chosen of God.
22.51: And the soldiers also mocked him, coming to him, and offering him vinegar,
22.52: And saying, If thou be the king of the Jews, save thyself.
22.53: And a superscription also was written over him in letters of Greek, and Latin, and Hebrew,
22.54: And one of the malefactors which were hanged railed on him, saying, If thou be Christ, save thyself and us.
22.55: But the other answering rebuked him, saying, Dost not thou fear God, seeing thou art in the same condemnation?
22.56: And we indeed justly; for we receive the due reward of our deeds: but this man hath done nothing amiss.
22.57: And he said unto Jesus, Lord, remember me when thou comest into thy kingdom.
22.58: And Jesus answering said to him – the one who rebuked – Courage! Verily I say unto thee, To day shalt thou be with me in paradise.
22.59: And it was about the sixth hour, and darkness came over the whole land until the ninth hour.
22.60: And the sun was darkened, and the veil of the sanctuary was rent in the midst.
22.61: And when Jesus had cried with a loud voice, he said, Father, into thy hands I commend my spirit: and having said this, he expired.
22.62: Now when the centurion saw what was done, he glorified God, saying, Certainly this man was righteous.
22.63: And all the multitude that came together to this sight, when they beheld the things which were done, returned smiting their breasts.
22.64: And all his acquaintance, and the women that followed him from Galilee, stood afar off, seeing these things.
22.65: The Jews therefore, because it was the preparation, that the bodies should not remain upon the cross on the sabbath day, for that sabbath day was an high day, besought Pilate that their legs might be broken, and that they might be taken away.
22.66: Then came the soldiers, and brake the legs of the first, and of the other which was crucified with him.
22.67: But when they came to Jesus, and saw that he was dead already, they brake not his legs:
22.68: But one of the soldiers with a spear pierced his side, and forthwith came there out blood and water.
22.69: And, behold, there was a man named Joseph, a counseller; and he was a good man, and a just:
22.70: The same had not consented to the counsel and deed of them; he was of Arimathaea, a city of the Jews: who also himself waited for the kingdom of God.
22.71: This man went unto Pilate, and begged the body of Jesus.
22.72: And he took it down, and wrapped it in a linen cloth, and laid it in a tomb that was hewn in stone, wherein no man had ever yet lain.
22.73: And it was the day of the preparation, and the sabbath was dawning.
22.74: And the women also, which had come with him from Galilee, followed after, and beheld the tomb, and how his body was laid.
22.75: And they returned, and prepared spices and ointments. And on the sabbath day they rested according to the commandment.

23.1: Now upon the first day of the week, very early in the morning, they came unto the sepulchre, bringing the spices which they had prepared, and certain others with them.
23.2: And they found the stone rolled away from the sepulchre.
23.3: And they entered in, and found not the body of the Lord Jesus.
23.4: And it came to pass, as they were much perplexed thereabout, behold, two men stood by them in shining garments:
23.5: And as they were afraid, and bowed down their faces to the earth, they said unto them, Why seek ye the living among the dead?
23.6: He is not here, but is risen: remember how he spake unto you when he was yet in Galilee,
23.7: Saying, The Son of man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men, and be crucified, and the third day rise again.
23.9: And returned from the sepulchre, and told all these things unto the eleven, and to all the rest.
23.10: It was Mary Magdalene, and Joanna, and Mary the mother of James, and other women that were with them, which told these things unto the apostles.
23.11: And their words seemed to them as idle tales, and they believed them not.
23.12: Then arose Peter, and ran unto the sepulchre; and stooping down, he beheld the linen clothes laid by themselves, and departed, wondering in himself at that which was come to pass.
23.13: And, behold, two of them went that same day to a village called Emmaus, which was from Jerusalem about threescore furlongs.
23.14: And they talked together of all these things which had happened.
23.15: And it came to pass, that, while they communed together and reasoned, Jesus himself drew near, and went with them.
23.16: But their eyes were holden that they should not know him.
23.17: And he said unto them, What manner of communications are these that ye have one to another, as ye walk, and are sad?
23.18: And the one of them, whose name was Cleopas, answering said unto him, Art thou only a stranger in Jerusalem, and hast not known the things which are come to pass therein these days?
23.19: And he said unto them, What things? And they said unto him, Concerning Jesus, which was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people:
23.20: And how the chief priests and our rulers delivered him to be condemned to death, and have crucified him.
23.21: But we trusted that it had been he which should have redeemed Israel: and beside all this, to day is the third day since these things were done.
23.22: Yea, and certain women also of our company made us astonished, which were early at the sepulchre;
23.23: And when they found not his body, they came, saying, that they had also seen a vision of angels, which said that he was alive.
23.24: And certain of them which were with us went to the sepulchre, and found it even so as the women had said: but him they saw not.
23.25: Then he said unto them, O foolish men, and slow of heart to believe in all that he spake to you.
23.26: Was it not neccessary for the Christ to have suffered these things, and to enter into his glory?
23.27: And they drew nigh unto the village, whither they went: and he made as though he would have gone further.
23.28: But they constrained him, saying, Abide with us: for it is toward evening, and the day is far spent. And he went in to tarry with them.
23.29: And it came to pass, as he sat at meat with them, he took bread, and blessed it, and brake, and gave to them.
23.30: And their eyes were opened, and they knew him; and he vanished out of their sight.
23.31: And they said one to another, Did not our heart burn within us, while he talked with us by the way, and while he opened to us the scriptures?
23.32: And they rose up the same hour, and returned to Jerusalem, and found the eleven gathered together, and them that were with them,
23.33: Saying, The Lord is risen indeed, and hath appeared to Simon.
23.34: And they rehearsed the things that happened in the way, and how he became known to them in the breaking of the bread.
23.35: And as they thus spake, Jesus himself stood in the midst of them, and saith unto them, Peace be unto you.
23.36: But they were terrified and affrighted, and supposed that they had seen a phantom.
23.37: And he said unto them, Why are ye troubled? and why do thoughts arise in your hearts?
23.38: Behold my hands and my feet, that it is I myself: for a spirit hath not flesh and bones, as ye see me have.
23.39: And when he had thus spoken, he shewed them his hands and his feet.
23.40: And while they yet believed not for joy, and wondered, he said unto them, Have ye here any meat?
23.41: And they gave him a piece of a broiled fish, and of an honeycomb.
23.42: And he took it, and did eat before them.
23.43: And he said unto them, These are the words which I spake unto you, while I was yet with you,
23.44: That thus it was neccessary for the Christ to suffer, and to rise again from the dead the third day:
23.45: And that repentance and remission of sins should be preached in his name among all nations.
23.46: And ye are witnesses of these things.
23.47: And, behold, I send the promise of my Father upon you: but tarry ye in the city of Jerusalem, until ye be endued with power from on high.

24.1: If ye love me, keep my commandments.
24.2: And I will pray the Father, and he shall give you another Comforter, that he may abide with you for ever;
24.3: Even the Spirit of truth; whom the world cannot receive, because it seeth him not, neither knoweth him: but ye know him; for he dwelleth with you, and shall be in you.
24.4: I will not leave you comfortless: I will come to you.
24.5: Yet a little while, and the world seeth me no more; but ye see me: because I live, ye shall live also.
24.6: At that day ye shall know that I am in my Father, and ye in me, and I in you.
24.7: He that hath my commandments, and keepeth them, he it is that loveth me: and he that loveth me shall be loved of my Father, and I will love him, and will manifest myself to him.
24.8: Judas saith unto him, not Iscariot, Lord, how is it that thou wilt manifest thyself unto us, and not unto the world?
24.9: Jesus answered and said unto him, If a man love me, he will keep my words: and my Father will love him, and we will come unto him, and make our abode with him.
24.10: He that loveth me not keepeth not my sayings: and the word which ye hear is not mine, but the Father’s which sent me.
24.11: These things have I spoken unto you, being yet present with you.
24.12: But the Comforter, which is the Holy Ghost, whom the Father will send in my name, he shall teach you all things, and bring all things to your remembrance, whatsoever I have said unto you.
24.13: Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you. Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.
24.14: Ye have heard how I said unto you, I go away, and come again unto you. If ye loved me, ye would rejoice, because I said, I go unto the Father: for my Father is greater than I.
24.15: And now I have told you before it come to pass, that, when it is come to pass, ye might believe.
24.16: Hereafter I will not talk much with you: for the prince of this world cometh, and hath nothing in me.
24.17: But that the world may know that I love the Father; and as the Father gave me commandment, even so I do. Arise, let us go hence.
24.18: I am the true vine, and my Father is the husbandman.
24.19: Every branch in me that beareth not fruit he taketh away: and every branch that beareth fruit, he purgeth it, that it may bring forth more fruit.
24.20: Now ye are clean through the word which I have spoken unto you.
24.21: Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine; no more can ye, except ye abide in me.
24.22: I am the vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without me ye can do nothing.
24.23: If a man abide not in me, he is cast forth as a branch, and is withered; and men gather them, and cast them into the fire, and they are burned.
24.24: If ye abide in me, and my words abide in you, ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you.
24.25: Herein is my Father glorified, that ye bear much fruit; so shall ye be my disciples.
24.26: If the world hate you, ye know that it hated me before it hated you.
24.27: If ye were of the world, the world would love his own: but because ye are not of the world, but I have chosen you out of the world, therefore the world hateth you.
24.28: Remember the word that I said unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord. If they have persecuted me, they will also persecute you; if they have kept my saying, they will keep yours also.
24.29: But all these things will they do unto you for my name’s sake, because they know not him that sent me.
24.30: If I had not come and spoken unto them, they had not had sin: but now they have no cloak for their sin.
24.31: He that hateth me hateth my Father also.
24.32: If I had not done among them the works which none other man did, they had not had sin: but now have they both seen and hated both me and my Father.
24.33: But this cometh to pass, that the word might be fulfilled that is written in their law, They hated me without a cause.
24.34: But when the Comforter is come, whom I will send unto you from the Father, even the Spirit of truth, which proceedeth from the Father, he shall testify of me:
24.35: And ye also shall bear witness, because ye have been with me from the beginning.
24.36: These things have I spoken unto you, that ye should not be offended.
24.37: They shall put you out of the synagogues: yea, the time cometh, that whosoever killeth you will think that he doeth God service.
24.38: And these things will they do unto you, because they have not known the Father, nor me.
24.39: But these things have I told you, that when the time shall come, ye may remember that I told you of them. And these things I said not unto you at the beginning, because I was with you.
24.40: These words spake Jesus, and lifted up his eyes to heaven, and said, Father, the hour is come; glorify thy Son, that thy Son also may glorify thee:
24.41: As thou hast given him power over all flesh, that he should give eternal life to as many as thou hast given him.
24.42: And this is life eternal, that they might know thee the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom thou hast sent.
24.43: I have glorified thee on the earth: I have finished the work which thou gavest me to do.
24.44: And now, O Father, glorify thou me with thine own self with the glory which I had with thee before the world was.
24.45: I have manifested thy name unto the men which thou gavest me out of the world: thine they were, and thou gavest them me; and they have kept thy word.
24.46: Now they have known that all things whatsoever thou hast given me are of thee.
24.47: For I have given unto them the words which thou gavest me; and they have received them, and have known surely that I came out from thee, and they have believed that thou didst send me.
24.48: I pray for them: I pray not for the world, but for them which thou hast given me; for they are thine.
24.49: And all mine are thine, and thine are mine; and I am glorified in them.
24.50: And now I am no more in the world, but these are in the world, and I come to thee. Holy Father, keep through thine own name those whom thou hast given me, that they may be one, as we are.
24.51: While I was with them in the world, I kept them in thy name: those that thou gavest me I have kept, and none of them is lost, but the son of perdition; that the scripture might be fulfilled.
24.52: And now come I to thee; and these things I speak in the world, that they might have my joy fulfilled in themselves.
24.53: I have given them thy word; and the world hath hated them, because they are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.
24.54: I pray not that thou shouldest take them out of the world, but that thou shouldest keep them from the evil.
24.55: They are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.
24.56: Sanctify them through thy truth: thy word is truth.
24.57: As thou hast sent me into the world, even so have I also sent them into the world.
24.58: And for their sakes I sanctify myself, that they also might be sanctified through the truth.
24.59: Neither pray I for these alone, but for them also which shall believe on me through their word;
24.60: That they all may be one; as thou, Father, art in me, and I in thee, that they also may be one in us: that the world may believe that thou hast sent me.
24.61: And the glory which thou gavest me I have given them; that they may be one, even as we are one:
24.62: I in them, and thou in me, that they may be made perfect in one; and that the world may know that thou hast sent me, and hast loved them, as thou hast loved me.
24.63: Father, I will that they also, whom thou hast given me, be with me where I am; that they may behold my glory, which thou hast given me: for thou lovedst me before the foundation of the world.
24.64: O righteous Father, the world hath not known thee: but I have known thee, and these have known that thou hast sent me.
24.65: And I have declared unto them thy name, and will declare it: that the love wherewith thou hast loved me may be in them, and I in them.
24.66: And he led them out as far as to Bethany, and he lifted up his hands, and blessed them.
24.67: And it came to pass, while he blessed them, he was parted from them, and carried up into heaven.
24.68: And they worshipped him, and returned to Jerusalem with great joy:
24.69: And were continually in the temple, praising and blessing God.
24.71: Then the eleven disciples went away into Galilee, into a mountain where Jesus had appointed them.
24.72: And when they saw him, they worshipped him: but some doubted.
24.73: And Jesus came and spake unto them, saying, All power is given unto me in heaven and in earth.
24.74: Go ye therefore, and teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost:
24.75: Teaching them to observe all things whatsoever I have commanded you: and, lo, I am with you always, even unto the end of the world. Amen.

1.2: And all the brethren which are with me, unto the churches of Galatia:
1.3: Grace be to you and peace from God the Father, and from our Lord Jesus Christ,
1.4: Who gave himself for our sins, that he might deliver us from this present evil world, according to the will of God and our Father:
1.5: To whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.
1.6: I marvel that ye are so soon removed from him that called you into the grace of Christ unto another gospel:
1.7: Which is not another according to my gospel; but there be some that trouble you, and would pervert to a gospel different from that of Christ.
1.8: But though we, or an angel from heaven, preach any other gospel unto you than that which we have preached unto you, let him be accursed.
1.9: As we said before, so say I now again, If any man preach any other gospel unto you than that ye have received, let him be accursed.
1.10: For do I now persuade men, or God? or do I seek to please men? for if I yet pleased men, I should not be the servant of Christ.
1.11: But I certify you, brethren, that the gospel which was preached of me is not after man.
1.12: For I neither received it of man, neither was I taught it, but by the revelation of Jesus Christ.
1.13: For ye have heard of my conversation in time past in the Jews’ religion, how that beyond measure I persecuted the church of God, and wasted it:
1.14: And profited in the Jews’ religion above many my equals in mine own nation, being more exceedingly zealous of the traditions of my fathers.
1.15: But when it pleased God, who separated me from my mother’s womb, and called me by his grace,
1.16: To reveal his Son in me, that I might preach him among the heathen; immediately I conferred not with flesh and blood:
1.17: Neither went I up to Jerusalem to them which were apostles before me; but I went into Arabia, and returned again unto Damascus.
1.18: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to see Peter, and abode with him fifteen days.
1.19: But other of the apostles saw I none, save James the Lord’s brother.
1.20: Now the things which I write unto you, behold, before God, I lie not.
1.21: Afterwards I came into the regions of Syria and Cilicia;
1.22: And was unknown by face unto the churches of Judaea which were in Christ:
1.23: But they had heard only, That he which persecuted us in times past now preacheth the faith which once he destroyed.
1.24: And they glorified God in me.

2.1: Then fourteen years after I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas, and took Titus with me also.
2.2: And I went up by revelation, and communicated unto them that gospel which I preach among the Gentiles, but privately to them which were of reputation, lest by any means I should run, or had run, in vain.
2.3: But neither Titus, who was with me, being a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised:
2.4: And that because of false brethren unawares brought in, who came in privily to spy out our liberty which we have in Christ Jesus, that they might bring us into bondage:
2.5: To whom we gave place by subjection, no, not for an hour; that the truth of the gospel might continue with you.
2.6: But of these who seemed to be somewhat, whatsoever they were, it maketh no matter to me: God accepteth no man’s person: for they who seemed to be somewhat in conference added nothing to me:
2.7: But contrariwise, when they saw that the gospel of the uncircumcision was committed unto me, as the gospel of the circumcision was unto Peter;
2.8: For he that wrought effectually in Peter to the apostleship of the circumcision, the same was mighty in me toward the Gentiles:
2.9: And when James, Cephas, and John, who seemed to be pillars, perceived the grace that was given unto me, they gave to me and Barnabas the right hands of fellowship; that we should go unto the heathen, and they unto the circumcision.
2.10: Only they would that we should remember the poor; the same which I also was forward to do.
2.11: But when Peter was come to Antioch, I withstood him to the face, because he was to be blamed.
2.12: For before that certain came from James, he did eat with the Gentiles: but when they were come, he withdrew and separated himself, fearing them which were of the circumcision.
2.13: And the other Jews dissembled likewise with him; insomuch that Barnabas also was carried away with their dissimulation.
2.14: But when I saw that they walked not uprightly according to the truth of the gospel, I said unto Peter before them all, If thou, being a Jew, livest after the manner of Gentiles, and not as do the Jews, why compellest thou the Gentiles to live as do the Jews?
2.15: We who are Jews by nature, and not sinners of the Gentiles,
2.16: Knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the law, but by the faith of Jesus Christ, even we have believed in Jesus Christ, that we might be justified by the faith of Christ, and not by the works of the law: for by the works of the law shall no flesh be justified.
2.17: But if, while we seek to be justified by Christ, we ourselves also are found sinners, is therefore Christ the minister of sin? God forbid.
2.18: For if I build again the things which I destroyed, I make myself a transgressor.
2.19: For I through the law am dead to the law, that I might live unto God.
2.20: I am crucified with Christ: nevertheless I live; yet not I, but Christ liveth in me: and the life which I now live in the flesh I live by the faith of the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself for me.
2.21: I do not frustrate the grace of God: for if righteousness come by the law, then Christ is dead in vain.

3.1: O foolish Galatians, who hath bewitched you, that ye should not obey the truth, before whose eyes Jesus Christ hath been evidently set forth, crucified among you?
3.2: This only would I learn of you, Received ye the Spirit by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?
3.3: Are ye so foolish? having begun in the Spirit, are ye now made perfect by the flesh?
3.4: Have ye suffered so many things in vain? if it be yet in vain.
3.5: He therefore that ministereth to you the Spirit, and worketh miracles among you, doeth he it by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?
3.6: So then they which be of faith are blessed.
3.7: Learn that the just shall live by faith.
3.8: For as many as are under the Law are under a curse;
3.9: But, the man that doeth them shall live by them.
3.10: Christ hath redeemed us from the curse of the law, being made a curse for us: Cursed is everyone that hangeth upon a tree; but he that is of the promise is by the freewoman.
3.11: Therefore we received the blessing of the Spirit by faith.
3.12: Brethren, I speak after the manner of men;

4.1: Now I say, That the heir, as long as he is a child, differeth nothing from a servant, though he be lord of all;
4.2: But is under tutors and governors until the time appointed of the father.
4.3: Even so we, when we were children, were in bondage under the elements of the world:
4.4: But when the fulness of the time was come, God sent forth his Son,
4.5: To redeem them that were under the law, that we might receive the adoption of sons.
4.6: And because ye are God’s sons, He hath sent forth his Spirit into our hearts, crying, Abba, Father
4.7: Wherefore thou art no more a servant, but a son; and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ.
4.8: Howbeit then, when ye knew not God, ye did service unto them which by nature are no gods.
4.9: But now, after that ye have known God, or rather are known of God, how turn ye again to the weak and beggarly elements, whereunto ye desire again to be in bondage?
4.10: Ye observe days, and months, and times, and years.
4.11: I am afraid of you, lest I have bestowed upon you labour in vain.
4.12: Brethren, I beseech you, be as I am; for I am as ye are: ye have not injured me at all.
4.13: Ye know how through infirmity of the flesh I preached the gospel unto you at the first.
4.14: And my temptation which was in my flesh ye despised not, nor rejected; but received me as an angel of God, even as Christ Jesus.
4.15: Where is then the blessedness ye spake of? for I bear you record, that, if it had been possible, ye would have plucked out your own eyes, and have given them to me.
4.16: Am I therefore become your enemy, because I tell you the truth?
4.17: They zealously affect you, but not well; yea, they would exclude you, that ye might affect them.
4.18: But it is good to be zealously affected always in a good thing, and not only when I am present with you.
4.19: My little children, of whom I travail in birth again until Christ be formed in you,
4.20: I desire to be present with you now, and to change my voice; for I stand in doubt of you.
4.21: Tell me, ye that desire to be under the law, do ye not hear the law?
4.22: For it is written, that Abraham had two sons, the one by a bondmaid, the other by a freewoman.
4.23: But he who was of the bondwoman was born after the flesh; but he of the freewoman was by promise.
4.24: Which things are an allegory: for these are the two expositions; the one from the mount Sinai, which gendereth to bondage, which is Agar.
4.25: For this Agar is mount Sinai in Arabia, and answereth to Jerusalem which now is, and is in bondage with her children.
4.26: Another that gendereth above all principality and power and dominion, and every name that is named, not only in this world but also in that which is to come.
4.27: The holy church our mother, to whom we have made promise.
4.28: Which is the mother of us all.
4.29: So then, brethren, we are not children of the bondwoman, but of the free.

5.1: Stand fast therefore in the liberty wherewith Christ hath made us free, and be not entangled again with the yoke of bondage.
5.2: Behold, I Paul say unto you, that if ye be circumcised, Christ shall profit you nothing.
5.3: I testify that a man that is circumcised is a debtor to do the whole Law,
5.4: Christ is become of no effect unto you, whosoever of you are justified by the law; ye are fallen from grace.
5.5: For we through the Spirit wait for the hope of righteousness by faith.
5.6: For in Jesus Christ neither circumcision availeth anything, nor uncircumcision; but faith which worketh by love.
5.7: Ye did run well; who did hinder you that ye should not obey the truth?
5.8: This persuasion cometh not of him that calleth you.
5.9: A little leaven corrupteth the whole lump.
5.10: I have confidence in you through the Lord, that ye will be none otherwise minded: but he that troubleth you shall bear his judgment, whosoever he be.
5.11: And I, brethren, if I yet preach circumcision, why do I yet suffer persecution? then is the offence of the cross ceased.
5.12: I would they were even cut off which trouble you.
5.13: For, brethren, ye have been called unto liberty; only use not liberty for an occasion to the flesh, but by love serve one another.
5.14: For all the Law is fulfilled by you; thou shalt love thy neighbor as thyself.
5.15: But if ye bite and devour one another, take heed that ye be not consumed one of another.
5.16: This I say then, Walk in the Spirit, and ye shall not fulfill the lust of the flesh.
5.17: For the flesh lusteth against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh: and these are contrary the one to the other: so that ye cannot do the things that ye would.
5.18: But if ye be led of the Spirit, ye are not under the law.
5.19: Now the works of the flesh are manifest, which are these; Adultery, fornication, uncleanness, lasciviousness,
5.20: Idolatry, witchcraft, hatred, variance, emulations, wrath, strife, seditions, heresies,
5.21: Envyings, murders, drunkenness, revellings, and such like: of the which I tell you before, as I have also told you in time past, that they which do such things shall not inherit the kingdom of God.
5.22: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith,
5.23: Meekness, temperance: against such there is no law.
5.24: And they that are Christ’s have crucified the flesh with the affections and lusts.
5.25: If we live in the Spirit, let us also walk in the Spirit.
5.26: Let us not be desirous of vain glory, provoking one another, envying one another.

6.1: Brethren, if a man be overtaken in a fault, ye which are spiritual, restore such an one in the spirit of meekness; considering thyself, lest thou also be tempted.
6.2: Bear ye one another’s burdens, and so fulfil the law of Christ.
6.3: For if a man think himself to be something, when he is nothing, he deceiveth himself.
6.4: But let every man prove his own work, and then shall he have rejoicing in himself alone, and not in another.
6.5: For every man shall bear his own burden.
6.6: Let him that is taught in the word communicate unto him that teacheth in all good things.
6.7: Be not deceived; God is not mocked: for whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap.
6.8: For he that soweth to his flesh shall of the flesh reap corruption; but he that soweth to the Spirit shall of the Spirit reap life everlasting.
6.9: And let us not be weary in well doing: for in due season we shall reap, if we faint not.
6.10: As we have therefore opportunity, let us do good unto all men, especially unto them who are of the household of faith.
6.11: Ye see how large a letter I have written unto you with mine own hand.
6.12: As many as desire to make a fair shew in the flesh, they constrain you to be circumcised; only lest they should suffer persecution for the cross of Christ.
6.13: For neither they themselves who are circumcised keep the law; but desire to have you circumcised, that they may glory in your flesh.
6.14: But God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom the world is crucified unto me, and I unto the world.
6.15: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision availeth anything, nor uncircumcision, but a new creature.
6.16: And as many as walk according to this rule, peace be on them, and mercy, and upon the Israel of God.
6.17: From henceforth let no man trouble me: for I bear in my body the marks of the Lord Jesus.
6.18: Brethren, the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.

1.1: Paul, called to be an apostle of Jesus Christ through the will of God, and Sosthenes our brother,
1.2: Unto the church of God which is at Corinth, to them that are sanctified in Christ Jesus, called to be saints, with all that in every place call upon the name of Jesus Christ our Lord, both theirs and ours:
1.3: Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.
1.4: I thank my God always on your behalf, for the grace of God which is given you by Jesus Christ;
1.5: That in every thing ye are enriched by him, in all utterance, and in all knowledge;
1.6: Even as the testimony of Christ was confirmed in you:
1.7: So that ye come behind in no gift; waiting for the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ:
1.8: Who shall also confirm you unto the end, that ye may be blameless in the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
1.9: God is faithful, by whom ye were called unto the fellowship of his Son Jesus Christ our Lord.
1.10: Now I beseech you, brethren, by the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that ye all speak the same thing, and that there be no divisions among you; but that ye be perfectly joined together in the same mind and in the same judgment.
1.11: For it hath been declared unto me of you, my brethren, by them which are of the house of Chloe, that there are contentions among you.
1.12: Now this I say, that every one of you saith, I am of Paul; and I of Apollos; and I of Cephas; and I of Christ.
